sowing.method: Planting of rooted cuttings or air-layered saplings in prepared pits

seed_rate.notes: Optimal vine density of 400-500 plants per hectare under 5m x 4m layout spacing.

sowing.soil_conditions: Well-drained gravelly or sandy loam soils with pH 6.5-7.5. Tolerant to dry conditions.

irrigation.notes: Provide light, regular drip irrigation to establish root network.
Drought stress induced for 1 month, followed by heavy watering to trigger uniform flowering.
Maintain consistent moisture. Irregular watering causes severe fruit cracking.

fertilizer.notes: Mix fertilizers with farmyard manure and neem cake.

fertilizer.method: Drip fertigation or soil ring application
fertilizer.notes: High potassium boosts fruit weight, rind coloring, and sweetness.

maturity_indicators: Rind turns from dark green to yellowish-red or bright red
Fruit shapes become angular instead of round, and metallic sound is heard when tapped
harvest.harvest_method: Hand clipping of fruits using sharp secateurs
harvest.notes: Harvest in early morning. Wrap fruits in tissue paper and pack in ventilated cartons.
